zh.Shevington, Lancashire, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾分頓
zh.Shavington cum Gresty, Cheshire, England: 英國爾沙文頓 姆 格雷斯迪
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor於1430年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾分頓時，他的父親John Woodward29歲，母親Margery Weaver25歲。他於1450年與'Agnes' Anne Corbet在英國英格蘭藍卡爾分頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1499年在英國爾米德列維其享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor于1430年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾分頓时，他的父亲John Woodward29岁，母亲Margery Weaver25岁。他于1450年与'Agnes' Anne Corbet在英国英格兰藍卡爾分頓结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1499年在英国爾米德列維其享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: Woodward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a forester employed to look after the trees and game in a forest, Middle English woodward (from Old English wudu ‘wood’ + weard ‘guardian, protector’).

History: Henry Woodward emigrated from England in 1635 and settled first in Dorchester, MA, and subsequently in Northampton, MA. He had many prominent descendants. Another Henry Woodward, born c. 1646 in the British West Indies, was the first English settler in SC (1664).

variants.name: Woodard
meanings: from the Norman Old French personal name Odard, Udard, Middle English Wodard, Wudard. It derives from the ancient Germanic name Authard, Othard, Odard, composed of the elements aud- ‘riches' + hard- ‘hard, bold’.
occupational name from Middle English wode ‘wood’ + herde ‘herd’ (Old English wudu + hierde), for a person who tended animals pastured in woodland.

variants.name: Woodworth
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Woodward or Wadworth, the latter being a habitational name from Wadworth in Yorkshire, which is derived from the Old English personal name Wada, Wadda + Old English worth ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Woodman
meanings: occupational name for a woodcutter or a forester (compare Woodward ), from Middle English wodeman ‘woodman’. In some instances this may be a variant of Wadman .
from the Middle English personal name Wudemann (Old English Wuduman, from wudu ‘wood’ + mann ‘man’).

nameMeanings.name: Shevington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Shevington in Lancashire, with intrusive -r-. This place probably gets its name from an ancient Brittonic word related to Welsh cefn ‘ridge’, with the later addition of Old English tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

nameMeanings.name: Manor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Ménard (see Menard ), reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending.

variants.name: Mayner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French and Middle English personal name Mainer, from ancient Germanic Maginhari, composed of the elements magin ‘strength, might’ + hari, heri ‘army’. Compare Mainer , Maynor , Manor , and Mannor .
Americanized form of German   Mainer  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor was born in 1430, in Shevington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Woodward, was 29 and his mother, Margery Weaver, was 25. He married 'Agnes' Anne Corbet in 1450, in Shevington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1499, in Middlewich, Cheshire, England, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor est né en 1430, à Shevington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Woodward, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margery Weaver, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé 'Agnes' Anne Corbet en 1450, à Shevington, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1499, à Middlewich, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor 1430 in Shevington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Woodward 29 und seine Mutter Margery Weaver 25. Er hat 1450 in Shevington, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom 'Agnes' Anne Corbet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1499 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Middlewich, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor è nato il nel 1430 nel Shevington, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Woodward, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Margery Weaver, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato 'Agnes' Anne Corbet nel 1450, nel Shevington, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1499, nel Middlewich, Cheshire, England, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manorが1430年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアシェヴィントンで生まれた時，父John Woodwardは29歳，母Margery Weaverは25歳であった。1450年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアシェヴィントンで'Agnes' Anne Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1499年にイギリスチェシュアイアミッドルイチで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor(이)가 1430년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 셰빙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Woodward(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Weaver(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1450년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 셰빙턴에서 'Agnes' Anne Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1499년 영국 체실르 미들루이치에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor родился в 1430 г. в местности шевингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Woodward было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Weaver, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени 'Agnes' Anne Corbet в 1450 г. в местности шевингтон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1499 г. в местности миддльвич, чешир, Англия в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord George Woodward               Shevington Manor of Shavinton Manor nació en 1430, en Shevington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Woodward, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Margery Weaver, tenía 25 años. Se casó con 'Agnes' Anne Corbet en 1450, en Shevington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1499, en Middlewich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 69 años.
